meta.name: GHQ-12 General Health Questionnaire
meta.description: The GHQ-12 is a widely used screening instrument for detecting non-psychotic psychiatric disorders and general psychological distress. It consists of 12 items covering feelings of strain, depression, inability to cope, anxiety-based insomnia, and lack of confidence.
meta.author: Goldberg & Williams (1988)
meta.reference: Goldberg DP, Williams P. A User's Guide to the General Health Questionnaire. Windsor: NFER-Nelson; 1988.
meta.instruction: We would like to know how your health has been in general over the past few weeks. Please answer all questions by selecting the answer which you think most nearly applies to you.

questions.text: Been able to concentrate on what you're doing?
Felt you were playing a useful part in things?
Felt capable of making decisions about things?
Felt you couldn't overcome your difficulties?
Been able to enjoy your normal day-to-day activities?
Been able to face up to your problems?
Been thinking of yourself as a worthless person?
Been feeling reasonably happy, all things considered?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, suggesting no significant psychological distress. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current healthy lifestyle
Keep up regular exercise, social activities, and sleep habits
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: Mild Distress
ranges.description: Your score suggests some degree of psychological distress that may benefit from attention. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Pay attention to your emotional well-being and identify sources of stress
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing or meditation
Maintain social connections and talk to someone you trust
Consider seeking professional advice if symptoms persist
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Significant Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates significant psychological distress. Professional support is recommended. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Consult a mental health professional for further assessment
Prioritize self-care and reduce unnecessary stressors
Maintain regular routines for sleep, meals, and physical activity
Reach out to trusted friends, family, or a crisis service if needed
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
